title: synth_xilinx crashes on FDSE with unconnected S port during XILINX_DFFOPT
body:
### Version

Yosys 0.50+7 (git sha1 38f858374, clang++ 14.0.0-1ubuntu1.1 -fPIC -O3)

### On which OS did this happen?

Linux

### Reproduction Steps

Create a Verilog file top.v with the following content:
```
module top(
    input wire clk,
    input wire data,
    output reg q
);
    FDSE fdse_inst (
        .C(clk),    // Clock
        .D(data),   // Data input
        .CE(1'b1),  // Clock enable
        .Q(q)      // Output
        // .S (synchronous set) port is left unconnected
    );
endmodule
```
Run Yosys with the following command:
`yosys -p "read_verilog top.v; synth_xilinx -flatten -nowidelut -arch xc7 -top top; json -o synth.json" `
Observe the crash during the XILINX_DFFOPT pass.

### Expected Behavior

If the S port of the FDSE primitive is required, Yosys should throw a clear error message indicating that the port is missing.

If the S port is optional, Yosys should either:

Silently ignore the unconnected port.

Assign a default value (e.g., S = 0 to disable the set function).

### Actual Behavior

Yosys crashes with the following error:
```
2.46. Executing XILINX_DFFOPT pass (optimize FF control signal usage).
Optimizing FFs in top.
terminate called after throwing an instance of 'std::out_of_range'
  what():  dict::at()
Aborted (core dumped)
```
